reportMetadata.title: Phase 2 – loadAll Refactor: Wiki Core Group Deep Analysis
reportMetadata.subtitle: Feasibility of Partial Refactor for sidebar, renderForm, renderDetailView, wikiTiptapEditors
reportMetadata.methodology: Four independent sub-agents (Call Graph Mapper, Dependency Matrix, Side-Effect Inventory, Risk Verdict) analyzed the code; results consolidated by orchestrator with strict multi-step validation.

dataFlow.summary: Complete map of every function that reads or writes each shared state variable in the wiki closure.

readFunctions.name: renderSidebarContent
readFunctions.detail: iterates entries to group by category and build tree

readFunctions.name: buildWikiTree
readFunctions.detail: receives catEntries and allEntries to build parent-child tree

readFunctions.name: renderWikiTreeLevel
readFunctions.detail: receives allEntries for cycle detection and entry lookups

readFunctions.detail: reads entries via parent lookups in parent link handler (line 13229)

readFunctions.name: handleSave

variables.note: BUG: Declared and read at line 14002, but NEVER assigned `= true` anywhere in the closure. The guard `if (pendingWikiMutation) return` is dead code.

readFunctions.name: sidebar ondrop (main)
readFunctions.detail: reads to prevent concurrent drops (but never true)

readFunctions.name: loadWikiCache
readFunctions.detail: reads cache to return cached entries

readFunctions.name: scanWikiRefs
readFunctions.detail: reads cache for wiki entry refs scan

readFunctions.name: editor renderWikiDetailView
readFunctions.detail: reads cache for parent entry lookup

keyObservations: entries is the most-touched variable — 11 read sites, 3 write sites, 5 mutate-in-place (all DnD)
formMetadata is the most complex object — 12+ write sites, many from TipTap editor callbacks
pendingWikiMutation is declared but NEVER set to true — dead guard code at line 14002
currentDragZone is written but never read — likely dead code
formParentId (singular) is written but never read — superseded by formParentIds (plural array)

dependencyMatrix.summary: Matrix of dependencies between the four Wiki Core sections. Minimum atomic unit for refactoring: {renderForm + wikiTiptapEditors}.

reasons: handleSave() iterates wikiTiptapEditors array (lines 12296-12315) to call editor.getHTML()
removeCustomSubcategory() iterates wikiTiptapEditors to destroy specific editor (lines 12590-12595)
initNotepadEditor() writes formMetadata.private_notepad and sets wikiFormDirty (lines 12790-12791)
Editor orchestration in render() (lines 14361-14460) reads formCategory, customSubcats, formMetadata.subcategories
form DOM (#wiki-subcat-*, #wiki-private-notepad) must exist before editor init — execution-order dependency

extractionCandidates.sidebar: Loose coupling to renderForm (reads entries for tree, DnD calls render()). Candidate for independent extraction.
extractionCandidates.renderDetailView: Cleanest section — no tight dependencies. window.renderWikiDetailView() already exported at line 13675.
extractionCandidates.renderForm+wikiTiptapEditors: Minimum atomic unit. Cannot be extracted independently.

sideEffects.summary: Inventory of all side-effects in the Wiki Core code, categorized by section, with assessment of whether they can be moved to init or replaced by delegation.

effects.alternative: Use event delegation on .wiki-sidebar — single click handler checks e.target.closest('.wiki-entry-item') and reads data-entry-id
Delegate to .wiki-sidebar — single dragstart handler reads data-entry-id
Delegate to .wiki-sidebar — single dragend handler clears indicators
Delegate to .wiki-sidebar — detect click on .wiki-category-header via closest()
Delegate to .wiki-sidebar — extend existing ondragover to handle category-header too

effects.sideEffect: Sidebar div ondragover handler (zone detection, indicator management)
effects.alternative: ALREADY using event delegation. Could be attached once at init instead of recreated on every render().

effects.sideEffect: Sidebar div ondrop handler (full DnD move/merge logic)
effects.alternative: ALREADY using event delegation. Contains optimistic UI + API calls. Could be attached once at init.

effects.sideEffect: TipTap editor destroy/create cycle (cleanup + re-init on every form render)
effects.alternative: With full DOM replacement: essential. With partial updates: existing dedup guards (lines 12729, 12772) prevent double-init. Skip cleanupWikiEditors() if DOM nodes preserved. Staggered retries (50/250/700/1500ms + 300ms) work around race conditions.
Only form section needs re-rendering. Sidebar is unchanged. Could render form HTML to fragment and swap into .wiki-main.
Only form section needs re-render (template changes affect subcategory structure).

effects.sideEffect: Async fetchTokenCount() via scheduleTokenFetch() on live token count
effects.alternative: Already debounced at 500ms. Display-only side-effect. Could be throttled to only fire when form is visible.

effects.sideEffect: Post-render async token fetch for detail view (wiki-detail-counts)
effects.alternative: Display-only. Could be deferred or lazy-loaded.

effects.sideEffect: Post-render DOM value assignment (formName, formContent, formCategory, snippet)
effects.alternative: Mostly REDUNDANT. Values already set in template (value: formName at line 13301, textContent at line 13461). Only formParentId reset (line 14356) serves a purpose. 3 of 4 can be removed.

effects.sideEffect: Inline onclick handlers on parent chip remove buttons and custom subcat rename/delete buttons
effects.alternative: Could use event delegation on .wiki-form container with robust data-attribute matching.

pureFunctions.name: computeWikiEntryCounts
pureFunctions.verdict: Pure — reads entry object, counts words/tokens, returns {wc, tc}. No mutation.

pureFunctions.name: computeWikiFormCounts
pureFunctions.verdict: Pure — reads component-level vars, returns {wc, tc}. No mutation.

pureFunctions.name: buildWikiTree
pureFunctions.verdict: Pure — creates local childrenMap + topLevel. No caching or external mutation.

pureFunctions.name: scanWikiRefs
pureFunctions.verdict: Pure — reads wikiEntriesCache (read-only), sorts a copy. Called only from chapter editor, NOT from wiki page.

strategyA.name: Extract all four sections together as single independent module
strategyA.performanceGain: High (~200-500ms per loadAll call if full API calls avoided)
crossDependencies: All 25+ closure variables must be refactored into a shared store — any desync breaks UI
All event handlers referencing closure state must be rewired
loadAll() and all callers must be updated
Sidebar DnD handlers reference entries, dragWikiEntryId, pendingWikiMutation — must be store-aware
window.renderWikiDetailView and window.renderWikiLanding already partially extracted — must unify
risks: TipTap editor lifecycle (destroy at cleanupWikiEditors, create at initWikiSubcategoryEditor) deeply interleaved with form DOM
DnD center-zone handler already does optimistic sidebar-only updates — contradicts 'unified render' approach
render() at 250+ lines orchestrates ALL sections — atomic extraction has no intermediate verification points
Staggered editor retries (50/250/700/1500ms + safety nets) would need careful re-timing against store initialization

strategyB.name: Extract only the sidebar behind a flag
strategyB.performanceGain: Medium (~50-150ms per DnD operation by avoiding full render + TipTap re-init)
crossDependencies: Must extract entries store (or pass as parameter)
computeWikiEntryCounts() must be extractable from closure (already pure)
DnD handlers on wiki-sidebar div (lines 13903-14251) must be re-attachable without full render()
Category header DnD (lines 13017-13061) calls render() — needs retargeting to sidebar-only update
risks: Sidebar DnD handlers reference entries, dragWikiEntryId, projectId — must pass explicitly or via store
Category header ondrop calls render() — must be changed to sidebar-only update
renderSidebarContent() is already self-contained (reads entries, calls pure helpers) — low risk
Center-zone DnD (lines 14101-14185) already demonstrates sidebar-only re-render pattern — can use as template

strategyC.name: Extract renderDetailView (already partially done)
strategyC.performanceGain: Low-Medium (~20-50ms per detail view render by avoiding full render())
crossDependencies: Depends on entries array (passed as parameter — fine)
Depends on collapsedSubcategories (passed as parameter — fine)
Depends on computeWikiEntryCounts() (closure function — should be extracted to shared utility)
risks: window.renderWikiDetailView (line 13675) is string-based parallel implementation of DOM-based renderDetailView (line 13078) — dual maintenance burden
Event delegation for collapse/expand/parent navigation/edit buttons patched after innerHTML insertion — could be cleaner
Flag window.usePartialUpdates defaults to false (line 11951) — nothing uses extracted version in production

rationale: Strategy A (extract all four) is too risky — the TipTap editor lifecycle and 25 shared variables create too many interdependencies for atomic extraction

Strategy B (sidebar only) has the best risk/reward ratio — renderSidebarContent() is already self-contained, center-zone DnD already proves the pattern works (lines 14101-14185), and sidebar re-renders are the most frequent operation (every DnD, every category toggle)
Strategy C (detail view) is already partially done but provides minimal isolated gain — detail view doesn't change frequently
Strategy B can be done incrementally across 5 commits without a big-bang merge
The sidebar is the 'read-mostly' section — it reads entries for display but rarely modifies them directly (only DnD ordering). This makes it the safest candidate for independent extraction
preconditions: Extract computeWikiEntryCounts() from closure to shared utility (pure, zero side-effects, can be window-scoped or module-scoped)
Extract buildWikiTree() from closure to shared utility (already pure, no side-effects)
Create wikiSidebarStore = { entries, projectId, dragWikiEntryId, categories } — a minimal store object the sidebar module reads from, populated by loadAll()
Ensure window.usePartialUpdates flag is togglable and defaults to false for safe rollout

commitBoundaries.name: Extract pure helpers
commitBoundaries.description: Extract computeWikiEntryCounts, buildWikiTree to shared window scope — 0 behavioral change

commitBoundaries.name: Create window.renderWikiSidebar()
commitBoundaries.description: Create HTML-string parallel to renderSidebarContent() — mirror of how renderWikiDetailView relates to renderDetailView

commitBoundaries.name: Wire sidebar into render() under flag
commitBoundaries.description: Attach event handlers via delegation (not inline per element) under window.usePartialUpdates flag

commitBoundaries.name: Convert center-zone DnD to sidebar-only update
commitBoundaries.description: Already partially done at lines 14143-14168 — make consistent
commitBoundaries.risk: Medium (optimistic UI must match server state)

commitBoundaries.name: Convert category-header drops to sidebar-only update
commitBoundaries.description: Replace full render() + loadAll() with sidebar-only update for category moves
commitBoundaries.risk: Medium (category change cascades to form state)

rollbackStrategy: Keep window.usePartialUpdates = false as default. The original DOM-based render() function and renderSidebarContent() remain unchanged. Toggle to true for testing. If sidebar extraction causes regressions, flip back to false. The sidebar module is loaded as an alternative render path, not a replacement.

painPoints.mostCoupledFunction: render() at line 13847 (~250 lines) — renders topbar, sidebar, form OR detail view, fires token fetch, sets post-render DOM values, initializes TipTap editors with staggered retries
painPoints.centerZoneDnDReason: Lines 14101-14185 already demonstrate the correct pattern: optimistic splice, sidebar.innerHTML replacement, API call, server reconciliation. Left/right zone fallback (lines 14188-14242) should be migrated to same pattern.
painPoints.simplestFirstStep: Extract computeWikiEntryCounts() to window scope (line 11979) — 0 behavioral change, instantly makes window.renderWikiDetailView() self-sufficient, takes 10 minutes, cannot break
painPoints.mostDangerousPart: TipTap editor lifecycle (cleanupWikiEditors → initWikiSubcategoryEditor → initNotepadEditor) with staggered retry cascade (50/250/700/1500ms + 100/300ms safety nets). If sidebar extraction accidentally triggers form re-render, editors are destroyed/re-created, losing cursor position, undo history, potentially leaking orphaned instances. 6 functions reference wikiTiptapEditors array — any refactor must preserve lifecycle consistency.

answers.q1_howManyFunctionsReadWriteEntries: 11 functions read entries, 3 functions write entries (reassign), 5 functions mutate entries in-place (all drag-and-drop operations). Total distinct functions touching entries: ~14.
answers.q2_mostCoupledFunction: render() at line 13847 (~250 lines) — orchestrates all four sections, inline handlers, post-render DOM setup, async token fetch, and TipTap editor initialization with staggered retries.
answers.q3_centerZoneDnDIsolatable: YES. The center-zone handler (lines 14101-14185) already demonstrates the correct optimistic sidebar-only update pattern. It splices entries in-place, replaces sidebar.innerHTML, and reconciles with server response. The left/right zone fallback (lines 14188-14242) still calls full loadAll() and should be migrated.
answers.q4_simplestFirstStep: Extract computeWikiEntryCounts() from closure to window scope. Pure function, zero side-effects, immediately makes window.renderWikiDetailView() self-sufficient, takes ~10 minutes, zero risk.
answers.q5_estimatedTotalPerformanceGain: If all four sections extracted successfully: ~200-500ms saved per loadAll() call by avoiding full API re-fetches and full DOM rebuild. If sidebar-only extracted: ~50-150ms saved per DnD operation by avoiding TipTap destroy/create cycle and full render().
answers.q6_mostDangerousPart: The TipTap editor lifecycle. The staggered retry cascade (50/250/700/1500ms + safety nets) creates a fragile timing dependency between DOM rendering and editor initialization. 6 functions read/write wikiTiptapEditors. Any code path that accidentally triggers cleanupWikiEditors() in the wrong order can orphan editor instances, lose cursor position, corrupt undo history, or cause memory leaks. The fact that pendingWikiMutation guard is broken (never set to true) exacerbates this — concurrent DnD operations and form state changes have no protection.
